resources.name: Teach Yourself Complete Croatian
resources.why: The most complete structured self-study course for Croatian, and one of the few high-quality ones that exists. It introduces the case system gradually with audio, dialogues and exercises — ideal scaffolding for a language with few good resources.

resources.name: Easy Croatian (YouTube)
resources.why: Real street interviews with subtitles in Croatian and English. It trains your ear on how natives actually speak and gives you authentic, current vocabulary — comprehensible input you can't get from a textbook.

resources.name: Anki + a Croatian frequency deck
resources.why: Spaced repetition is even more important for Croatian because vocabulary overlap with English is low. A frequency-ordered deck ensures the ~600 words that do most of the work become automatic. 15 minutes a day compounds fast.

resources.name: HiNative / Croatian subreddit & forums
resources.why: Croatian grammar questions (especially cases and aspect) are best answered by natives. The r/Croatian community and HiNative give fast, friendly corrections — invaluable when textbooks are thin and tutors are scarce.

resources.name: italki — a native Croatian tutor
resources.why: Speaking practice is the bottleneck for Croatian, and a tutor is the single fastest fix. Two 30-minute conversation lessons a week force output, drill the cases in context, and build confidence no app can.
